report_name: MoneyForward GitHub Breach Exposes Customer Data, Hardcoded Secrets

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Attackers
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actors responsible for compromising MoneyForward's corporate GitHub account between May 1 and May 3, 2026, leading to the theft of source code and exposure of customer data.
threat_actors.goals: Theft of source code, sensitive data, and authentication keys for potential future attacks or data sales.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: Attackers gained access to hardcoded authentication keys found within the source code files of MoneyForward's compromised GitHub repository.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Attackers copied and exfiltrated company source code and a file containing personal data of 370 users from the compromised corporate GitHub repository.

identities.name: MoneyForward Inc.
identities.description: A prominent Japanese financial technology provider that suffered a data breach via its corporate GitHub account, exposing source code, hardcoded keys, and customer data.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The cloud-based source code repository service whose platform hosted the compromised MoneyForward repository.

infrastructure.name: Compromised GitHub Repository
infrastructure.description: MoneyForward's corporate GitHub repository that was compromised, allowing attackers to access and exfiltrate source code and sensitive data that had been improperly committed.

indicators.name: Anomalous GitHub Repository Clone or Download
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous GitHub Repository Clone or Download
id: 2b9c1f8a-3d7e-4b1f-8c9a-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Monitors for anomalous repository cloning from a GitHub repository, which could indicate a repository compromise and source code exfiltration as seen in the MoneyForward breach.
references:
  - https://www.pasqualepillitteri.it/moneyforward-github-hack-stolen-code-and-370-cards-exposed-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
logsource:
  product: github
  service: audit
detection:
  selection:
    action: 'repo.clone'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate developer cloning a repository for the first time.
  - Automated systems cloning repositories as part of a CI/CD process.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects GitHub audit log events for repository cloning. A spike in cloning activity or clones from unusual IP addresses or users could indicate a breach similar to the one at MoneyForward, where attackers copied source code.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous GitHub Repository Clone or Download
